from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    fluent, natural‑language description.  The implementation uses richer
    templates, handles direction‑reversed predicates (e.g. backupPilot where the
    object is the person and the subject is the mission), and builds a short
    paragraph by joining clauses with periods instead of a single comma‑list.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from literal strings.
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('\"')

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is the astronaut.
    astronaut_templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member of {clean(o)}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)} citizen",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the award {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} earned {clean(o)} awards",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is a mission (or other entity)
    # and the object is a person or organization.
    mission_templates = {
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} was operated by {clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {clean(s)}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the commander of {clean(s)}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a crew member of {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the representative of {clean(s)}",
    }

    # Build clauses, grouping mission‑related roles and astronaut attributes.
    clauses = []
    mission_roles = {}   # {mission: {predicate: [object, ...]}}
    astronaut_info = {}  # {subject: {predicate: [object, ...]}}
    # Generic templates for non‑astronaut, non‑mission predicates.
    generic_templates = {
        "bird": lambda s, o: f"The {clean(o)} is a bird of {clean(s)}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the leader of {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the representative of {clean(s)}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is also known as {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} received {clean(o)} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} received the award {clean(o)}",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is part of {clean(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is part of {clean(o)}",
        "fossil": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is known for the fossil {clean(o)}",
        "gemstone": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} is known for the gemstone {clean(o)}",
        "mascot": lambda s, o: f"The mascot of {clean(s)} is {clean(o)}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} was operated by {clean(o)}",
    }

    for triple in triples:
        if triple.predicate in astronaut_templates:
            astronaut_info.setdefault(triple.subject, {}).setdefault(triple.predicate, []).append(triple.object)
        elif triple.predicate in mission_templates:
            mission_roles.setdefault(triple.subject, {}).setdefault(triple.predicate, []).append(triple.object)
        elif triple.predicate in generic_templates:
            clauses.append(generic_templates[triple.predicate](triple.subject, triple.object))
        else:
            # Fallback generic handling.
            clauses.append(f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}")

    # Build astronaut sentences (one per subject) using a logical order.
    astronaut_order = ["status", "birthName", "birthDate", "birthPlace", "nationality",
                       "almaMater", "occupation", "title", "selectedByNasa",
                       "mission", "timeInSpace", "dateOfRetirement", "deathDate", "deathPlace", "awards", "award", "ribbonAward"]
    for subject, attrs in astronaut_info.items():
        parts = []
        # Start with subject name.
        sentence_start = clean(subject)
        # Status is usually a short adjective.
        if "status" in attrs:
            status_val = clean(attrs["status"][0]).lower()
            parts.append(status_val)
        # Birth name if present.
        if "birthName" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"originally named {clean(attrs['birthName'][0])}")
        # Occupation.
        if "occupation" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"was a {clean(attrs['occupation'][0])}")
        # Birth place and date together.
        if "birthPlace" in attrs and "birthDate" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"born in {clean(attrs['birthPlace'][0])} on {clean(attrs['birthDate'][0])}")
        else:
            if "birthPlace" in attrs:
                parts.append(f"born in {clean(attrs['birthPlace'][0])}")
            if "birthDate" in attrs:
                parts.append(f"born on {clean(attrs['birthDate'][0])}")
        # Nationality.
        if "nationality" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"was a {clean(attrs['nationality'][0])} citizen")
        # Alma mater.
        if "almaMater" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"studied at {clean(attrs['almaMater'][0])}")
        # Selected by NASA.
        if "selectedByNasa" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"was selected by NASA in {clean(attrs['selectedByNasa'][0])}")
        # Mission(s).
        if "mission" in attrs:
            missions = [clean(m) for m in attrs["mission"]]
            parts.append(f"served on mission{'s' if len(missions)>1 else ''} {', '.join(missions)}")
        # Time in space.
        if "timeInSpace" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"spent {clean(attrs['timeInSpace'][0])} minutes in space")
        # Retirement.
        if "dateOfRetirement" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"retired in {clean(attrs['dateOfRetirement'][0])}")
        # Death.
        if "deathDate" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"died on {clean(attrs['deathDate'][0])}")
        if "deathPlace" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"died in {clean(attrs['deathPlace'][0])}")
        # Awards.
        if "awards" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"earned {clean(attrs['awards'][0])} awards")
        if "award" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"received the award {clean(attrs['award'][0])}")
        if "ribbonAward" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"received the {clean(attrs['ribbonAward'][0])} ribbon")
        # Assemble the sentence.
        if parts:
            # Insert commas, and add the subject at the beginning.
            sentence = sentence_start + ", " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
            clauses.append(sentence)
        else:
            clauses.append(sentence_start + ".")

    # Helper to build a phrase for a role.
    role_phrases = {
        "commander": lambda person, mission: f"{person} was the commander of {mission}",
        "crewMembers": lambda person, mission: f"{person} was a crew member of {mission}",
        "backupPilot": lambda person, mission: f"{person} was a backup pilot for {mission}",
        "representative": lambda person, mission: f"{person} was the representative of {mission}",
        "operator": lambda org, mission: f"{mission} was operated by {org}",
    }

    # Create combined sentences for each mission.
    for mission, roles in mission_roles.items():
        parts = []
        # Preserve a logical order of roles.
        for pred in ["commander", "crewMembers", "backupPilot", "representative", "operator"]:
            objs = roles.get(pred, [])
            for obj in objs:
                phrase_func = role_phrases.get(pred)
                if phrase_func:
                    parts.append(phrase_func(clean(obj) if pred != "operator" else clean(mission), clean(mission) if pred != "operator" else clean(obj)))
        if parts:
            # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last part.
            if len(parts) == 1:
                combined = parts[0]
            else:
                combined = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
            clauses.append(combined)

    # Join all clauses into a single sentence.
    sentence = ". ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
